response.justification: James is a strong systems engineer with recent, relevant agentic-AI infrastructure experience at re:cinq and proven expertise in high-performance distributed systems. However, this CTO role requires demonstrated executive leadership (10+ years with 5+ in senior technical leadership, proven team scaling to 30+ people), fintech domain expertise, and production multi-agent systems in regulated environments. His resume shows strong technical depth but lacks C-level executive experience, any fintech background, and evidence of team scaling at the required scope. His agentic-AI infrastructure work, while timely, is too recent (2 months) to evidence production readiness at the scale and responsibility this role demands.
strengths: Currently building agentic frameworks and MCP infrastructure at re:cinq, directly aligned with the technical AI architecture requirements
Deep distributed-systems expertise with proven work on high-throughput systems (millions of requests/second at Dunnhumby, event-driven architectures, real-time inference)
Polyglot engineer comfortable across backend, infrastructure, and full-stack domains with hands-on experience in DevOps, CI/CD, and system design
concerns: No executive leadership background; resume shows Staff and Senior Engineer roles but no CTO, VP Engineering, or Head of Technical positions scaling organizations to 30+ people
Zero fintech experience; no background with credit systems, payment flows, algorithmic working capital, risk modeling, or regulated financial environments

Agentic-AI production systems experience is nascent (current role since April 2025); lacks demonstrated track record at scale and no evidence of LLM selection, fine-tuning, or evaluation methodology expertise
